evidence_summary: Both IL-27 and IL-36α act on CD4+ T cells independently (IL-27 inhibits Th17/induces IL-10; IL-36α drives colitogenic Th cells) but no published evidence shows IL-27 induces IL-36α expression.
citations: Interleukin-27 in T Cell Immunity — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4346869/
IL-36 cytokines imprint a colitogenic phenotype on CD4+ T helper cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9038530/
search_queries_used: IL-27 induces IL-36 alpha CD4 T cells
"IL-27" "IL-36" cascade induction

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: decorin induces VEGF and modulates VEGFR2; VEGF participates in decorin down-regulation (autocrine feedback) rather than induction. Not described in CD8 T cells.
citations: Ectopic decorin expression up-regulates VEGF expression in mouse cerebral endothelial cells — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18021292/
Decorin (Wikipedia) — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decorin
search_queries_used: VEGF induces decorin expression
VEGF Decorin proteoglycan TGF-beta CD8 T cells

evidence_summary: VEGF predominantly suppresses T cell function (via VEGFR2) and induces IL-6/IL-8/GRO-α in endothelium, not leukocytes. No evidence VEGF induces IL-22 in MAIT cells; IL-22 induction in MAIT is driven by IL-7/IL-12/IL-18/IL-23.
citations: VEGF directly suppresses activation of T cells from ascites — https://www.nature.com/articles/bjc2012468
VEGF MAIT search — https://immunenetwork.org/DOIx.php?id=10.4110%2Fin.2026.26.e8
search_queries_used: VEGF induces IL-22 T cells
VEGF MAIT cells cytokine production IL-22

evidence_summary: APRIL/TNFSF13 mainly acts on B cells (class switching, plasma cell survival, IgA+ Bregs) and was found to regulate IL-17/Th2 responses; no published evidence APRIL induces IL-22 in MAIT cells.
citations: APRIL (TNFSF13) regulates collagen-induced arthritis, IL-17 production and Th2 response — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/19016524/
search_queries_used: APRIL TNFSF13 induces IL-22 MAIT cells

evidence_summary: Tregs express LTα1β2 (membrane lymphotoxin) and use LTβR signaling for migration/tumor crosstalk, but no published evidence LTα2β1 induces IL-9 in Tregs.
citations: Regulatory T cells crosstalk with tumor cells and endothelium through lymphotoxin signaling — https://www.nature.com/articles/s41467-024-54874-y
search_queries_used: lymphotoxin LT alpha2 beta1 induces IL-9 Treg
"LT alpha beta" induces IL-9 lymphotoxin

evidence_summary: Type-I IFNs (IFN-α/β) act as 'signal 3' on CD8 T cells; IFN-α subtypes induce significantly higher frequencies of IL-2-expressing CD8+ T cells (>2-fold increase) and synergize with IL-2 signaling for effector differentiation.
citations: Diverse Immunomodulatory Effects of Individual IFNα Subtypes on Virus-Specific CD8+ T Cell Responses — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2019.02255/full
Regulation of Effector and Memory CD8 T Cell Differentiation by IL-2 — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6306427/
search_queries_used: IFN-beta induces IL-2 CD8 T cells

evidence_summary: IL-29/IFN-λ1 induction is canonically driven by viral/dsRNA stimuli through IRF/NF-κB pathways. No published evidence IL-9 induces IFN-λ1 in CD8 T cells.
citations: Interleukin 29 (Wikipedia) — https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Interleukin_29
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces IFN-lambda IL-29 expression
IL-9 promotes IL-29 lambda interferon production

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-27 inhibits IL-22 production by upregulating SOCS1 in CD4+ T cells. No evidence IL-22 induces IL-27 in MAIT cells.
citations: Interleukin-22 Influences the Th1/Th17 Axis — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2021.618110/full
Identification of IL-27 as a novel regulator of MHC class I/II — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC10561092/
search_queries_used: IL-22 induces IL-27 MAIT
"IL-22 stimulates IL-27" OR "IL-22 induces IL-27"

evidence_summary: CD30L/CD153 is expressed on activated T/B/NK cells and is CD40-inducible; no published evidence IL-9 induces CD30L expression on HSPCs.
citations: CD30 Is a CD40-Inducible Molecule — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4621001/
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces CD30L CD153 HSPC hematopoietic

evidence_summary: Noggin is a BMP antagonist not classically considered an immune cytokine. Noggin/BMP signaling affects T cell IL-2 transcription, but APRIL has no documented role in inducing Noggin in CD8 T cells.
citations: Differential effects of inhibition of BMP signalling on T-cell activation — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22144105/
search_queries_used: APRIL induces Noggin BMP T cells
APRIL induces noggin BMP antagonist immune

evidence_summary: CD30L/CD30 signaling is critical for maintenance and activation of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells in mucosa-associated tissues; CD30 stimulation directly induces IL-17 production in human cells (in vitro RA studies). Documented for γδ rather than CD8 specifically.
citations: CD30L/CD30 is critical for maintenance of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells — https://www.nature.com/articles/mi201318
CD30 Is Required for Activation of IL-17A-producing γδ T cells in Innate Immunity — https://journals.asm.org/doi/10.1128/iai.00887-13
search_queries_used: CD30L induces IL-17A T cells

evidence_summary: IL-36Ra is a negative regulator that blocks IL-36R signaling. No published evidence IL-36Ra induces IL-27 in MAIT cells; the relationship would be expected to be indirect (release of inhibition).
citations: IL-36 cytokines in inflammatory and malignant diseases — https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00018-021-03909-4
search_queries_used: IL-36Ra induces IL-27 MAIT cells

evidence_summary: IL-29/IFN-λ1 up-regulates IL-6 production in PBMCs, macrophages, and synovial fibroblasts; IFN-λ directly responds in CD8 T cells. Direct induction in CD8 T cells specifically is not as well documented but plausible.
citations: IL-29 enhances Toll-like receptor-mediated IL-6 and IL-8 production — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3978693/
Modulation of the human cytokine response by IFN-λ1/IL-29 — https://www.nature.com/articles/6364348
search_queries_used: IFN-lambda IL-29 induces IL-6 CD8 T cells
"IFN-lambda" "IL-29" CD8 T cell IL-6 induction

evidence_summary: CD40L is upregulated by IL-2, IL-12, IL-15 and downregulated by IL-4, IL-10; CD40L is expressed on activated T cells, not classically on cDCs. No published evidence IL-19 induces CD40L on cDCs.
citations: The role of CD40 and CD40L in Dendritic Cells — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2749083/
search_queries_used: IL-19 induces CD40L dendritic cells
IL-19 upregulates CD40L T cell help

evidence_summary: C5a promotes IL-22 and IL-17 expression and provides costimulatory/survival signals to CD4+ T cells; IL-36α is produced by CD4+ T cells in inflammation. No direct evidence C5a induces IL-36α specifically, but pathways converge on CD4 T cell inflammation.
citations: Complement component C5a Promotes Expression of IL-22 and IL-17 from Human T cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3154861/
Elevated IL-36α And CD4+IL-36α+T Cells in Graves' Disease — https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6190875/
search_queries_used: C5a complement induces IL-36 alpha CD4 T cells

evidence_summary: Documented direction is reverse: IL-4 induces APRIL expression in macrophages via p38MAPK-CREB/Stat6. No published evidence APRIL induces IL-4 in NKT cells.
citations: IL-4 stimulates mouse macrophages to express APRIL through p38MAPK and two different downstream molecules — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S1043466609001069
APRIL stimulates NF-κB-mediated HoxC4 induction for AID expression in mouse B cells — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3723325/
search_queries_used: APRIL induces IL-4 NKT cells
APRIL stimulates IL-4 B cells immune

evidence_summary: IL-9 acts on bronchial epithelial cells (BEAS-2B) in vitro to induce the T-cell chemoattractants IL-16 and RANTES; IL-16 can be induced by IL-4/IL-9/TNF/serotonin. Documented in epithelial cells, not NKT specifically.
citations: Interleukin (IL)-4/IL-9 and Exogenous IL-16 Induce IL-16 Production by BEAS-2B Cells — https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0008874900917453
Role of interleukin-16 in human diseases — https://www.frontiersin.org/journals/immunology/articles/10.3389/fimmu.2025.1524026/full
search_queries_used: IL-9 induces IL-16 lymphocyte chemoattractant

evidence_summary: Noggin is a BMP antagonist not classically considered an immune cytokine. IL-6 has many monocyte effects (MDSC induction, MCP-1 induction) but no published evidence IL-6 induces Noggin expression in CD14+ monocytes.
citations: IL-6 Induces Monocyte Chemotactic Protein-1 in PBMCs and U937 — https://ashpublications.org/blood/article/91/1/258/260347/Interleukin-6-Induces-Monocyte-Chemotactic-Protein
search_queries_used: IL-6 induces Noggin monocyte expression

evidence_summary: TL1A is induced by IL-1β/TNFα and synergizes with IL-12/IL-18 for NK IFN-γ/cytotoxicity. IL-36α drives Th1 polarization but no published evidence IL-36α induces TL1A in NK CD56bright cells.
citations: TL1A selectively enhances IL-12/IL-18-induced NK cell cytotoxicity — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20349123/
IL-36 signaling amplifies Th1 responses — https://ashpublications.org/blood/article/120/17/3478/30630/IL-36-signaling-amplifies-Th1-responses-by
